summary: Twenty years of building what doesn’t exist yet. Self-taught technologist who follows the same cycle at every job: arrive, get hands-on, observe what’s broken, reimagine the workflow, then build the technology that fixes it. This process has made me consistently replace teams of 4–10 people as a solo operator — from presenting a national digital currency plan to Egypt’s Central Bank in 2006 to designing a mobility platform with multiple novel concepts no competitor has built.
thinking1.title: My Process: Arrive → Observe → Reimagine → Build
thinking1.sub: THE MASTER PATTERN · EVERY JOB · 20 YEARS
thinking1.body:
THE CYCLE

Across 20 years and every role, the same cycle repeats: I enter a new environment with zero assumptions. I get my hands dirty — doing the actual work, feeling the friction. Then my mind starts analyzing: why is this slow? Why does this break? What’s the bottleneck?

Once I see the friction, I can’t unsee it. I start imagining a different workflow — better quality, faster speed, more logical structure. Then I study how technology can close the gap.

THE PROOF — EVERY SINGLE JOB

IM Solutions: Arrived at call center → saw hosting team struggling → self-taught servers at night → sites in 5 days vs 1 month

TMT Egypt: Arrived at operations → zero cost visibility → built Excel tracking the owner called impossible → saved more than my salary

AI Training: Arrived as transcriber → 1-2 week review cycles → built self-learning QA sheet → #1 in quality and productivity

PC Company: Arrived at assembly → unoptimized software, no tracking → built activation system, price monitoring, repurposed support software into order tracker

Commuting daily: Experienced exhausting friction → imagined a journey autopilot → designed CommutePilot

I enter a system. I see what’s broken. I build what fixes it.
thinking2.title: I See Systems Before They Exist
thinking2.body:
THE MOMENT

In 2006, as a university student, I mapped out how Egypt’s entire economy could transition from cash to digital over 14 years. A complete phased plan with banking infrastructure and merchant adoption.

Presented to Egypt’s Central Bank. Nothing happened. Two years later, Bitcoin launched. A decade later, every central bank started CBDC programs.

Same period: independently conceived metamaterial cloaking (Duke University demonstrated it months later) and solar-pumped lasers (real technology now).

PATTERN: Ideas arrive 10–20 years before the market
thinking3.title: I Build Self-Learning Systems From Nothing
thinking3.body:
THE PROBLEM

Arabic transcription for AI training. Every file: transcribe → upload → wait → auditor finds mistakes → redo. One file: 1–2 weeks.

MY SOLUTION

Built a self-learning QA system in Excel. Fed every error. The sheet auto-checked future work against all known patterns.

99% accuracy. #1 productivity. Tried to share — team couldn’t believe it existed.

PATTERN: I automate quality before anyone asks
thinking4.title: I Solve “Impossible” Problems With Existing Tools
thinking4.body:
THE “IMPOSSIBLE” PROBLEM

Owner needed labor cost per project. Workers moved between sites daily. “Impossible to calculate.”

MY APPROACH

Excel distributing worker hours using supervisor data. Zero to exact cost breakdown. First audit caught significant overpayments.

Later: smart home alternative at 70–80% less with MORE functionality. At the PC company, repurposed IT support software as order fulfillment tracker — because tracking requests and tracking orders are the same workflow.

PATTERN: The tool exists. I see a different use for it.
thinking5.title: I Protect Quality Even When Nobody Asks
thinking5.body:
WHAT I DISCOVERED

A shadow economy: Europeans registered for $18/hr AI projects, outsourced to developing countries at $4/hr. Training data for Google, Apple, Microsoft, Amazon was terrible.

WHAT I DID

Reported it. Nobody listened. Built my own operation: writers, trainers, QC team, remote monitoring, custom browser extensions, intensive exams.

PRINCIPLE: “I prefer to be poor before anyone gets hurt because of me”
thinking6.title: I Run 10 Things Simultaneously
thinking6.body:
THE PROOF

4 simultaneous calls — only one capable. Clients requested by name. Self-taught hosting at night. Joomla — sites in 5–7 days vs 1 month. 10 parallel projects.

After leaving, senior dev (paid 7–8× more) couldn’t complete a single project.

AFTERMATH: Two companies needed 4+ people to replace me
project.commutepilot.desc: Commute autopilot. 7-signal sensor fusion works underground. Dynamic delivery to predicted locations. Invisible transit mapping. No competitor has this.
project.oem.desc: Windows license management. MySQL + PHP REST API + PowerShell. Bcrypt, audit logging, sessions. Built with Claude Code.
job1.title: Product & Systems Specialist

job1.detail2: Developed price monitoring tool (Google Apps Script, v8.5) tracking 40+ hardware brands with timeout protection and robust part number extraction
job1.detail3: Repurposed an IT support ticketing system as a complete order fulfillment tracker with custom mobile UI — saw that tracking support requests and tracking orders are the same workflow
job1.detail4: Created Windows deployment workflows using NTLite golden images with driver integration for Intel RST/VMD environments

job6.detail1: LINKdotNET: Improvised infrastructure solutions on ADSL opening day that senior technicians said they’d never have thought of

vault.commutepilot.body:
A commute autopilot app. You set your multi-leg journey once — train, bus, taxi — and the app runs it every day. Sleep on transit and wake up at your stop via a 7-signal sensor fusion alarm that works underground where GPS fails. Your coffee is pre-ordered and timed to your arrival. Your taxi is pre-booked. Groceries are collected on your route home.

Novel concepts include: dynamic delivery to your predicted future location, invisible transit mapping from phone sensor data, universal road surface quality mapping, and a delay cascade engine that recalculates your entire chain the moment anything changes.

No existing app combines any of these capabilities
vault.kidcart.body:
A marketplace designed for children’s products — but with a layer no competitor has: divorce spending verification. When parents are separated, one parent can verify that money sent for the child is actually spent on the child. Transparent receipts, category tracking, approval workflows.

Also includes age-appropriate product filtering, parental approval for purchases, and educational content integration.

Addresses a real pain point for millions of divorced families worldwide
vault.lockers.body:
Double-door lockers for restaurants: kitchen loads from the back, customer picks up from the front. Temperature-controlled — heated for hot food, cooled for cold items. No human interaction needed. No waiting in line.

Designed for the gap between restaurant kitchen and delivery/pickup. Reduces wait times, eliminates order mix-ups, works 24/7.

Bonus feature #126 — my daughter’s idea: a vibrating seat alarm for when your order is ready.

Transforms restaurant pickup into a vending machine experience
vault.currency.title: National Digital Currency
vault.currency.body:
A 14-year phased plan to transition Egypt from cash to digital payments. Included banking infrastructure changes, merchant adoption strategy, and citizen onboarding — presented to a representative from the National Bank of Egypt and Central Bank in 2006.

This was 2 years before Bitcoin, a decade before Apple Pay, and 15+ years before any government launched a CBDC program. The concept mapped almost exactly onto what the world is building today.

Conceived 2006 · Bitcoin launched 2008 · CBDCs began ~2020
vault.cloaking.title: Metamaterial Cloaking
vault.cloaking.body:
Conceived the idea that materials could bend light around an object, making it invisible. Worked out the basic principle independently as a student in Egypt.

Months later, Duke University demonstrated the world’s first metamaterial cloak in their laboratory — validating the exact same concept.

Independent conception validated by Duke University in the same year
vault.solar.title: Solar-Pumped Laser
vault.solar.body:
Conceived a system to concentrate sunlight through lenses and fiber optics to pump a laser medium — converting raw sunlight into coherent laser energy without electricity.

This is now a real technology in active development by research labs worldwide for applications in space propulsion, manufacturing, and energy transmission.

Now an active research field in labs worldwide
vault.safety.title: Community Safety Platform
vault.safety.sub: LOST & FOUND · MISSING PERSONS · CRIME INTELLIGENCE · 2019
vault.safety.body:
Facebook is used for finding lost children, lost wallets, missing persons — but posts get buried in hours, there’s no geographic targeting, no age filtering, no permanence. Social media is not designed for safety.

I designed a specialized platform with multiple layers: a permanent, searchable lost & found database with geographic targeting. A missing child instant alert system — mother reports lost child in a mall, push notification with photo goes to every user within the geographic radius instantly. Thousands of eyes activated in seconds.

Beyond that: community intelligence gathering — anonymous reports of suspicious behavior that aggregate into patterns. 50 people report aggressive behavior from the same person → police gets an automatic flag. Behavioral pattern analysis from historical data to speed up crime investigation. Not accusations — intelligence to accelerate justice.

Similar to what Citizen App ($400M+ valuation) does in the US, but designed for developing countries where official systems are slower and communities are the real safety net.

Designed 2019 · Combines Citizen App + Amber Alert + community intelligence
vault.health.title: Global Healthcare Intelligence
vault.health.sub: UNIVERSAL RECORDS · DOCTOR RANKING · PATIENT MATCHING · 2019
vault.health.body:
People choose doctors based on fake reviews and friend recommendations. There’s no centralized system tracking diagnosis quality, treatment outcomes, or doctor performance. Patients are victims of random medical decisions with zero accountability.

I designed a platform for: universal medical records accessible worldwide — tourist in a foreign hospital, doctor sees your full history instantly. Doctor performance tracking by condition — success rates per doctor per medical case type. Patient-doctor matching based on real outcome data, not reviews.

Plus: diagnosis quality control — did the doctor run proper tests before prescribing? Global health intelligence from billions of aggregated cases — which treatments work for which demographics, early pandemic detection, pharmaceutical research acceleration.

No existing platform combines all these layers. Zocdoc does booking. Epic does records within one network. Nobody does global performance tracking + patient matching + quality control.

Designed 2019 · Universal records + doctor ranking + outcome-based matching
footer.quote: I design systems that don’t exist yet. Then I find ways to build them.
